title: Cordyline flabelliformis
taxonomy: Cordyline flabelliformis is classified as a species within the family Asparagaceae. It belongs to the genus Cordyline, a group of plants native to the Pacific Islands. Cordyline flabelliformis is further categorized under the subfamily Lomandroideae. This classification is based on its characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other species. The species is native to the islands of the Pacific, where it grows in a variety of habitats.

intro: Cordyline flabelliformis, also known as New Zealand cabbage tree or flax lily, is a species of flowering plant native to New Zealand. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cordyline flabelliformis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
